prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

import argparse
import os


def validate_restart_value(value):
    valid_restarts = ['no', 'on-success', 'on-failure', 'on-abnormal', 'on-watchdog', 'on-abort', 'always']
    if value not in valid_restarts:
        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"restart must be one of {valid_restarts}")
    return value


def generate_service(args):
    after_targets = args.after
    if isinstance(after_targets, str):
        after_targets = [after_targets]

    env_vars = args.env
    if env_vars:
        env_lines = '\n'.join([f'Environment={k}={v}' for k, v in env_vars])
    else:
        env_lines = ''

    service_content = f"""[Unit]
Description={args.description}
After={' '.join(after_targets)}

[Service]
ExecStart={args.exec}
User={args.user}
Restart={args.restart}
RestartSec={args.restart_sec}
{f'WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}' if args.working_dir else ''}

{env_lines}

[Install]
WantedBy={args.wanted_by}
"""

    output_file = args.output
    with open(output_file, 'w') as f:
        f.write(service_content)

    print(f'Successfully generated {output_file}')


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description='Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments',
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter
    )

    parser.add_argument('--name', required=True, help='Name of the service (used in filename if not specified with --output)')
    parser.add_argument('--exec', required=True, help='Executable command to run')
    parser.add_argument('--user', default='root', help='User to run the service as')
    parser.add_argument(
        '--description',
        default='{name} service',
        help='Description for the service unit'
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        '--after',
        nargs='+',
        default=['network.target'],
        help='Targets that this service should start after'
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        '--restart',
        type=validate_restart_value,
        default='on-failure',
        choices=['no', 'on-success', 'on-failure', 'on-abnormal', 'on-watchdog', 'on-abort', 'always'],
        help='Restart strategy for the service'
    )
    parser.add_argument('--restart-sec', type=int, default=5, help='Seconds to wait before restarting')
    parser.add_argument(
        '--output',
        default='{name}.service',
        help='Output file path for the generated service unit'
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        '--wanted-by',
        default='multi-user.target',
        help='Target to install this service into'
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        '--env',
        action='append',
        metavar='KEY=VALUE',
        help='Environment variables to set (can be specified multiple times)'
    )
    parser.add_argument('--working-dir', help='Working directory for the service')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    args.description = args.description.format(name=args.name)
    args.output = args.output.format(name=args.name)

    generate_service(args)


if __name__ == '__main__':

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse


def validate_restart_value(value):
    valid_restarts = [
        "no",
        "on-success",
        "on-failure",
        "on-abnormal",
        "on-watchdog",
        "on-abort",
        "always",
    ]
    if value not in valid_restarts:
        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"restart must be one of {valid_restarts}")
    return value


def generate_service(args):
    after_targets = args.after
    if isinstance(after_targets, str):
        after_targets = [after_targets]

    env_vars = args.env
    if env_vars:
        env_lines = "\n".join(
            [f"Environment={k}={v}" for k, v in [env.split("=", 1) for env in env_vars]]
        )
    else:
        env_lines = ""

    service_content = f"""[Unit]
Description={args.description}
After={" ".join(after_targets)}

[Service]
ExecStart={args.exec}
User={args.user}
Restart={args.restart}
RestartSec={args.restart_sec}
{f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}" if args.working_dir else ""}

{env_lines}

[Install]
WantedBy={args.wanted_by}
"""

    output_file = args.output
    with open(output_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(service_content)

    print(f"Successfully generated {output_file}")


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments",
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--name",
        required=True,
        help="Name of the service (used in filename if not specified with --output)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable command to run")
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root", help="User to run the service as")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        default="{name} service",
        help="Description for the service unit",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        nargs="+",
        default=["network.target"],
        help="Targets that this service should start after",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        type=validate_restart_value,
        default="on-failure",
        choices=[
            "no",
            "on-success",
            "on-failure",
            "on-abnormal",
            "on-watchdog",
            "on-abort",
            "always",
        ],
        help="Restart strategy for the service",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec", type=int, default=5, help="Seconds to wait before restarting"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default="{name}.service",
        help="Output file path for the generated service unit",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="Target to install this service into",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        metavar="KEY=VALUE",
        help="Environment variables to set (can be specified multiple times)",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory for the service")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    args.description = args.description.format(name=args.name)
    args.output = args.output.format(name=args.name)

    generate_service(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

[Unit]
Description=My Application
After=network.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/myapp
User=www-data
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5




[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

[Unit]
Description=Web Server Service
After=network.target sshd

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/start-webserver
User=nginx
Restart=on-success
RestartSec=10
WorkingDirectory=/var/www

Environment=PORT=8080
Environment=HOST=0.0.0.0

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
